# Deja Vu

> A familiarity-first memory engine for AI agents.

Deja Vu is a TypeScript memory core for agents that need long-term memory without always-on retrieval. It uses a three-layer design:

- familiarity index
- summary layer
- chunk layer

The engine is intended to be embedded into a host agent runtime. The host is responsible for persistence, namespace policy, selective writeback, and prompt injection.

## Best starting points

- [README](./README.md): project overview, public API, integration model, and usage examples
- [Architecture](./docs/architecture.md): engine flow and plugin seams
- [Agent Handshake](./docs/agent-handshake.md): how an agent should integrate this repository as a memory core
- [Plugin Types](./src/types/plugins.ts): embedding, storage, vector store, and scoring interfaces
- [Memory Types](./src/types/memory.ts): memory records, recall result types, and chunk taxonomy
- [Core Engine](./src/core/semantic-recall-engine.ts): reference implementation of add, recall, update, and delete flows

## Concepts

- familiarity-first recall
- semantic recall
- long-term memory for AI agents
- project memory
- selective context loading
- threshold-gated retrieval
- plugin-first memory architecture

## Intended use

Use Deja Vu when you want:

- single-project long-term memory
- compact prompt context
- a RAG alternative for agent continuity
- a host-managed memory bridge with persistent storage

Do not treat this repository as a complete autonomous memory operating system. It is a memory core that expects a host integration layer.
